title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-17 · 美伊谅解备忘录（MOU）争议
summary: CNN《今晨》报道美伊协议文本未公开，引发争议。副总统万斯称因外交原因暂不公布，以色列请求被拒。加拿大总理卡尼透露已阅协议并称赞。分析人士指出协议分两步，先解冻石油贸易，核问题留待后续谈判。此外，特朗普支持的候选人在初选中表现参差，得州小型飞机坠毁获路人救援，五人因策划袭击白宫拳击赛被捕。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: Her Facebook page has reached nearly 400,000 followers. And fans like to watch the bubbly bovine just hanging out on the front porch of her family's home. And life is about to get even more interesting for Daisy. Guess what? She's expected to deliver her first calf soon. Congratulations to the growing family. Well, that does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 她的Facebook主页已拥有近40万粉丝。粉丝们喜欢看这头活泼的奶牛在她家前廊闲逛。而对黛西来说，生活将变得更加有趣。猜猜怎么着？她很快就要生下第一头小牛了。恭喜这个不断壮大的家庭。好了，CNN新闻速递到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's the elephant in the room at the G-7 right now. What's in the text of Trump's agreement with Iran, and why hasn't the world seen it yet?
paragraphs.cn: 这是七国集团峰会眼下房间里的大象。特朗普与伊朗的协议文本内容是什么？为什么世界还没有看到？

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Pence
paragraphs.en: This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.
paragraphs.cn: 这份谅解备忘录带有绥靖的味道。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Dramatic moments on a Texas highway. Bystanders race to save passengers after a fiery plane crash.
paragraphs.cn: 德克萨斯州高速公路上发生戏剧性一幕。飞机起火坠毁后，旁观者奋力营救乘客。

paragraphs.speaker: Matthew Quinn
paragraphs.en: They were planning to attack the Freedom 250.
paragraphs.cn: 他们原计划袭击"自由250"活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Five people accused in a chilling plot to attack fight night on the White House lawn. How close were the suspects to actually pulling it off?
paragraphs.cn: 五人被指控策划在白宫草坪上袭击格斗之夜的骇人阴谋。嫌疑人距离实施计划有多近？

paragraphs.speaker: Kamala Harris
paragraphs.en: I have no question or doubt that we will win the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: 我毫不怀疑我们将在中期选举中获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Democrats have high hopes for the midterms, but a new CNN poll suggests culture wars could play an even bigger role this time around. And workers armed with bleach and tiny bubbles fight nature at the National Mall's reflecting pool. Was a blue paint job no match for an algae bloom?
paragraphs.cn: 民主党对中期选举寄予厚望，但一项新的CNN民调显示，文化战争这次可能发挥更大作用。工人们手持漂白剂和小气泡在国家广场的倒影池中与自然抗争。蓝色油漆涂层是否敌不过藻类爆发？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Wait, I need that line from "Wicked." You're green. Oh, it's green. SEN. CHUCK SCHUMER (D-NY): I'm calling on Trump to immediately tell the American people on whatever is in his so-called deal. He needs to stop keeping America in the dark.
paragraphs.cn: 等等，我需要《魔法坏女巫》里的那句台词。你是绿色的。哦，是绿色的。参议员查克·舒默（纽约州民主党）：我呼吁特朗普立即向美国人民说明他所谓的协议中到底有什么。他需要停止让美国蒙在鼓里。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right now, President Trump and his administration are attempting to shape the narrative around their proposal with Iran, as the world eagerly awaits the text of this memorandum of understanding. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the blame game this morning. Vice President J.D. Vance says, we want to tell you what's in the agreement, but we can't, at least not yet, he says.
paragraphs.cn: 目前，特朗普总统及其政府正试图围绕他们与伊朗的提案塑造舆论，而全世界都在急切等待这份谅解备忘录的文本。大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。今天早上我们从相互指责开始。副总统J.D.万斯说，我们想告诉你们协议的内容，但我们不能，至少现在还不能，他说。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: There are some delicate diplomatic things going on where the Iranians -- and not just the Iranians, but some of our mediators, the Pakistanis and the Qataris -- have asked us to sequence this in the right way.
paragraphs.cn: 有一些微妙的外交事务正在进行，伊朗方面 -- -- 不仅是伊朗，还有我们的一些调解方，巴基斯坦和卡塔尔 -- -- 要求我们以正确的方式安排顺序。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But it turns out Israel doesn't even know. A source telling CNN Israel requested to see the agreement, and that request was rejected. One person who has seen the MOU, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, and he spoke to CNN while at the G-7 summit.
paragraphs.cn: 但结果是以色列甚至不知情。有消息人士告诉CNN，以色列曾要求查看该协议，但被拒绝。看过这份谅解备忘录的人之一是加拿大总理马克·卡尼，他在G7峰会期间接受了CNN采访。

paragraphs.speaker: Mark Carney
paragraphs.en: I have to say, it's exceeded my expectations. We're very pleased with the deal that's been struck. It sets the groundwork for to ensure Iran doesn't have a nuclear weapon. It sets the groundwork for a reintegration over time of the economies in the region. It sets the groundwork for a solution in Lebanon.
paragraphs.cn: 我必须说，这超出了我的预期。我们对达成的协议非常满意。它为确保伊朗不拥有核武器奠定了基础，为地区经济逐步重新融合奠定了基础，也为解决黎巴嫩问题奠定了基础。

paragraphs.speaker: Kaitlan Collins
paragraphs.en: Did you actually see the agreement itself?
paragraphs.cn: 你确实看到了协议本身吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Carney
paragraphs.en: I've seen the agreement, yes.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我看过协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Collins
paragraphs.en: Oh, so the president showed it to you?
paragraphs.cn: 哦，所以是总统给你看的？

paragraphs.speaker: Carney
paragraphs.en: Well, I've seen the agreement. We have -- we have -- we have our sources. I think it's a game changer.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我看过协议。我们有 -- -- 我们有 -- -- 我们有我们的消息来源。我认为这是一个改变游戏规则的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now, CNN political and national security analyst David Sanger. Good morning, David. So, we have --
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们的是CNN政治与国家安全分析师大卫·桑格。早上好，大卫。那么，我们 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: David Sanger
paragraphs.en: Good morning, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 早上好，奥黛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we have -- good morning. There's no text publicly. But clearly, someone's got it. I was surprised to see Carney, who has not been the most sort of positive relationship with Trump, speak highly of it. Is that the sort of word out of the G-7 in terms of how European leaders are responding?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，我们 -- -- 早上好。目前没有公开的文本。但显然有人拿到了。我很惊讶看到卡尼 -- -- 他与特朗普的关系并不算特别积极 -- -- 对此高度评价。这是G7方面关于欧洲领导人如何回应的说法吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: Well, I think the European leaders are just delighted at this point that there is something that has relieved the immediate crisis, which is getting the oil flowing again across the Strait of Hormuz. And that would happen almost immediately under the text of -- of this agreement. What wouldn't happen immediately, though, is any actual resolution of the nuclear issues.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为欧洲领导人此刻很高兴，因为终于有办法缓解眼前的危机，即让霍尔木兹海峡的石油运输恢复。根据这份协议的文本，这几乎会立即实现。不过，不会立即实现的是核问题的任何实际解决方案。

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: That all goes to a second negotiation, one that I think is going to be a lot more difficult, though the president said yesterday he thought it would be relatively easy. And that's going to look a lot more like the kind of long slog you saw the Obama administration go through in 2015. And they ultimately got an accord, but it took them 18 months.
paragraphs.cn: 这一切都进入第二轮谈判，我认为这将困难得多，尽管总统昨天表示他认为会相对容易。这看起来更像奥巴马政府在2015年经历的那种漫长拉锯战。他们最终达成了协议，但花了18个月。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's interesting. You were writing about the way that the White House has been thinking about that Obama-era agreement. And I've noticed even in the president's Truth Social accounts, he's posting cartoons, sort of comparing Obama-era policy on Iran with his Arab policy on Iran. And, you know, I -- why does he, Obama, live like rent-free in the heads of those working at the White House? Like, what is the deal with constantly framing it this way?
paragraphs.cn: 有趣的是，你之前写到白宫对奥巴马时代协议的看法。我注意到，即使在总统的Truth Social账号上，他也在发布漫画，将奥巴马对伊朗的政策与他自己的阿拉伯政策进行比较。你知道，为什么奥巴马能如此轻松地占据白宫工作人员的心思？为什么总是这样来框定问题？

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: Yes, it's real -- No, it's really fascinating. I mean, obviously, the president is defensive about this, because he ended up killing the Obama-era agreement in 2018 and said it was the worst agreement ever. And specifically spoke to shortfalls that it had: that it didn't deal with missiles; that it expired after 15 years; that it allowed the Iranians to continue to do some low-level nuclear work.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, it's really intriguing. Obviously, the president is defensive about it because he terminated the Obama-era deal in 2018 and called it the worst deal ever. He specifically pointed out the deal's flaws: it didn't address missiles; it expired after 15 years; it allowed Iran to continue some low-level nuclear activities.

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: The problem he's got now is he has to go strike an agreement in this second negotiation that exceeds the Obama agreement in all of its elements. And maybe he'll be able to do that. He called me on -- on Sunday evening to argue that, in fact, that was sort of precooked, and it was on the way. And that -- that he would manage to go get a much broader agreement than Obama did.
paragraphs.cn: The problem he faces now is that he has to reach a deal in the second round of talks that surpasses the Obama deal in every aspect. Maybe he can do it. He called me on Sunday night and argued that it was actually pre-arranged and moving forward. He will try to get a broader deal than Obama's.

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: But we won't know until we've seen that next -- that next agreement.
paragraphs.cn: But until we see the next deal, we won't know.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I think the pressure is also coming. You know, the call's from inside the house. You have so many warhawks. You have Israel, a lot of people critiquing this. And in our introduction, we played this clip of Mike Pence calling it a kind of appeasement. I see J.D. Vance on the tour, on the media tour, going on "Megyn Kelly," trying to quiet these voices. Is the White House worried about it?
paragraphs.cn: I think the pressure is also increasing. You know, pressure from within. There are so many hawks. And Israel, many people criticize that. In our opening, we played a clip of Mike Pence calling it appeasement. I saw J.D. Vance on a media tour, on The Megyn Kelly Show, trying to calm those voices. Is the White House worried?

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: Well, they are worried about it. And look, I think we're going to see a copy of this. Already, there are various versions of this agreement, some of which look pretty close to right to me, based on my own reporting, that are floating around on the Internet. So, they're going to have to sort this out, probably by Friday when Vice President Vance is supposed to go and actually do a ceremonial signing and then start up this next round of negotiations. But there's something in here for everybody to dislike.
paragraphs.cn: They are worried. And I think we'll see a copy of the deal. Various versions have been circulating online, some of which, based on my reporting, look quite close. So, they have to resolve this by Friday, when Vice President Vance will attend the signing ceremony and then start the next round of talks. But there's always something in this deal that displeases everyone.

paragraphs.speaker: Sanger
paragraphs.en: The Israelis are not going to like the fact that it does not -- this agreement does not speak to Iran's support for proxies: Hezbollah and Hamas. The Iranians aren't going to like the fact that it does have a reference to them giving up their most highly enriched uranium. And many in the U.S. are going to say, well, what? Why are we rewarding the Iranians by lifting sanctions and giving them -- unfreezing their money, which is essentially the critique President Trump was making in 2016 when he ran for president.
paragraphs.cn: Israel won't like that the deal doesn't address Iran's support for proxies (Hezbollah and Hamas). Iran won't like that the deal mentions giving up its highest enriched uranium. And many Americans will say, why? Why should we reward Iran by lifting sanctions and unfreezing funds? That's basically the criticism Trump made when he ran in 2016.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's David Sanger. Thank you so much. Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, we're report on the small plane crash in Texas. And seconds later, a daring rescue from brave bystanders. Plus, Messi opens his final World Cup run with a history-making hat trick. And a mixed night for President Trump's candidates. Is his endorsement still the gold standard? REP. MIKE COLLINS (R-GA),
paragraphs.cn: Okay, that was David Sanger. Thank you very much. Coming up on CNN This Morning, we'll cover a small plane crash in Texas. Seconds later, brave bystanders made a daring rescue. Also, Messi began his final World Cup journey with a historic hat trick. And President Trump's candidates had a mixed night. Is his endorsement still the gold standard? Republican Representative Mike Collins from Georgia,

paragraphs.speaker: Georgia Senatorial Candidate
paragraphs.en: Sometimes, we've got some strong disagreements. But I can tell you one thing: Now we stand united around one mission.
paragraphs.cn: Sometimes, we have strong disagreements. But I can tell you one thing: now we are united around one mission.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: Every time I endorse, they win. It's been a pretty amazing track record,
paragraphs.cn: Every time I endorse, they win. It's a pretty remarkable record.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Actually, it was a mixed bag last night for the power of President Trump's endorsement. For instance, in Georgia, his pick for the Senate, Congressman Mike Collins, will win the GOP primary. That sets the stage for a high- stakes race in November against rising Democratic Party star Senator Jon Ossoff.
paragraphs.cn: Actually, last night was a mixed bag for the power of President Trump's endorsement. For example, in Georgia, the Senate candidate he endorsed, Representative Mike Collins, will win the Republican primary. This sets up a high-stakes showdown in November with rising Democratic star Senator Jon Ossoff.

paragraphs.speaker: Collins
paragraphs.en: You know, we're Republicans, now. We're going to have some robust primaries out there. Sometimes then, we've got some strong disagreements. But I can tell you one thing: now we stand united around one mission. It's to put a Republican in that seat and get rid of that Jon Ossoff in November, return this seat to the people of Georgia.
paragraphs.cn: You know, we are Republicans now. We'll have some tough primaries. Sometimes, we have strong disagreements. But I can tell you one thing: now we are united around one mission. That is to have a Republican hold that seat in November, get rid of Jon Ossoff, and give that seat back to the people of Georgia.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But the people of Georgia also rejected Trump's -- Trump- backed Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones, in the Republican runoff for governor, favoring billionaire Rick Jackson. RICK JACKSON (R),
paragraphs.cn: But the people of Georgia also rejected Trump-endorsed Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones, and in the Republican gubernatorial runoff, they favored billionaire Rick Jackson.

paragraphs.speaker: Georgia Gubernatorial Candidate
paragraphs.en: I'm the only candidate who doesn't owe a thing to the political establishment. I don't care what special interests want, how much they beg, how much they give me. I can't be bought. And I won't back down.
paragraphs.cn: I am the only candidate who doesn't owe anything to the political establishment. I don't care what special interests want, no matter how much they beg or how much money they give me. I can't be bought. And I won't back down.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, Jackson will face former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms in the November governor's race. So, joining me now in the group chat, Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter for "Semafor"; Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project; and Antjuan Seawright, Democratic strategist. Eleanor, can I turn to you to start? Just because there's so many races, frankly. I mean, we mentioned Georgia, but there was also the Alabama Senate.
paragraphs.cn: So, Jackson will face former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms in the November gubernatorial race. Now, joining our group chat are Eleanor Miller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor, Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project, and Democratic strategist Antwan Seawright. Eleanor, can I start with you? Because frankly, there are so many races. I mean, we mentioned Georgia, but there's also the Senate election in Alabama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: ELEANOR MUELLER, WHITE HOUSE ECONOMIC POLICY REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Eleanor Miller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor: Yes.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Where you had a GOP Senate runoff. A Trump-backed candidate, Barry Moore, is the winner. Then in Oklahoma, there was a governor's race. The Trump-endorsed Mike Mazzei finished second, headed for a runoff. Are voters -- are we looking at just local politics versus Trump, or is there something going on with a Trump-backed endorsement?
paragraphs.cn: There's a Republican Senate runoff there. Trump-endorsed candidate Barry Moore won. Then in Oklahoma, there's a gubernatorial race. Trump-endorsed Mike Mazzei came in second and will go to a runoff. Voters -- -- are we seeing local politics versus Trump, or is something wrong with Trump's endorsements?

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: It's a great question. I think in Georgia, you know, the results showed us that if there's one thing that can beat Trump in a Republican primary, it is money, right? The gubernatorial candidate, who is not Trump-endorsed, who won out, had spent $100 million of his own funds in this race. Clearly, that was effective. But I think it also echoes a dynamic we're seeing in places like the Texas Senate race, where, you know, the Trump-backed candidate may not be the most competitive against the Democratic candidate.
paragraphs.cn: That's a good question. I think in Georgia, the result tells us that if anything can beat Trump in a Republican primary, it's money, right? The gubernatorial candidate who didn't get Trump's endorsement spent $100 million of his own money on this race. Obviously, that worked. But I think it also echoes a dynamic we've seen in places like the Texas Senate election, where the Trump-endorsed candidate may not be the best to take on the Democratic candidate.

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: And so, we will see, come November, whether or not a candidate that can invigorate the Republican base --
paragraphs.cn: So, we'll see in November whether a candidate who can energize the Republican base -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: -- with the Trump endorsement is better than a candidate that can appeal to independents and a more traditional type of conservative.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- with Trump's endorsement, is better than a candidate who can attract independents and more traditional conservatives.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: When we're talking about Oklahoma and Alabama, that's one thing. Georgia to me is a special case because of its role in the 2020 election and the role Republicans there faced in rejecting the Trump administration at a time in its attempts to overturn the election. And between that and the Marjorie Taylor Greene of it all, it feels like the Republican politics there are a little more scrambled. So, is it the money? Is it also the local?
paragraphs.cn: When we talk about Oklahoma and Alabama, that's one thing. But for me, Georgia is a special case because of its role in the 2020 election and the fact that Republicans there faced rejection of the Trump administration's attempts to overturn the election results. Plus the factor of Marjorie Taylor Greene, it feels like Republican politics there is more chaotic. So, is it about money? Or local factors?

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: I think it's a little bit of everything. Obviously, I think we're seeing now that the -- what the value of a Trump endorsement is, and it's $100 million. But one thing I will point out is that Mike Collins is a solid MAGA candidate. He is "America first" all the way. And you could actually make the argument that, while in the gubernatorial race, the Trump- endorsed candidate didn't win, the Trump candidate won.
paragraphs.cn: I think it's a mix of factors. Obviously, we now see the value of a Trump endorsement -- -- up to $100 million. But I want to point out that Mike Collins is a solid MAGA candidate. He fully embraces 'America First.' Actually, you could argue that while the Trump-endorsed candidate didn't win in the gubernatorial race, Trump's candidate won.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, why wouldn't Trump back him?
paragraphs.cn: So why didn't Trump endorse him?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I -- well, look, there are all different types of reasons to endorse people. But Rick Jackson, if you listen to his comments last night, he's the outsider. He's the guy running on pro-family, family-centered economics. He wants to have the state income tax, like, for everyone.
paragraphs.cn: I -- -- well, you know, endorsements happen for various reasons. But Rick Jackson, if you listened to his comments last night, he's an outsider. He advocates pro-family, family-centered economic policies. He wants everyone to pay state income tax.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Here's the thing. He's not -- you're talking about Rick Jackson.
paragraphs.cn: The thing is, he's not -- -- you're talking about Rick Jackson.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Here's what he's running on, though. I want to play an ad, because what you're saying is very interesting. But this is what he told the voters.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。不过，这是他的竞选主张。我想播放一则广告，因为你说的话很有意思。但这是他告诉选民的。

paragraphs.speaker: Jackson
paragraphs.en: I don't care if you're Muslim or Mongolian, you don't have the right to force your culture on our country.
paragraphs.cn: 我不在乎你是穆斯林还是蒙古人，你无权将你的文化强加给我们的国家。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Conservative Rick Jackson.
paragraphs.cn: 保守派里克·杰克逊。

paragraphs.speaker: Jackson
paragraphs.en: Too often, criminal illegals commit sick, violent crimes, victimize our children, and get away with murder. So, here's my guarantee to them: do that when I'm governor, and you'll end up deported or departed.
paragraphs.cn: 很多时候，非法移民罪犯犯下令人发指的暴力罪行，伤害我们的孩子，却逍遥法外。所以，我向他们保证：如果我当州长，你们再这样做，要么被驱逐出境，要么就离开人世。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Rick Jackson for Georgia.
paragraphs.cn: 里克·杰克逊，代表佐治亚州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Set aside the death threat at the end of that? I don't know if there's a Mongolian human trafficking ring going on, but what I hear is culture war, culture war. It was not gas prices. It was not affordability. It wasn't any of those things. Antjuan, is the return of a culture war argument a weakness for Democrats?
paragraphs.cn: 先不管结尾的死亡威胁？我不知道有没有蒙古人口贩卖团伙，但我听到的是文化战争，文化战争。不是油价，不是负担能力，也不是其他任何问题。安特万，文化战争论调的回归对民主党来说是弱点吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.en: I don't know. Look, I can summarize these primaries.
paragraphs.cn: 我不知道。听着，我可以总结这些初选。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You never say, "I don't know." Do you know that? Like, that is -- that's a telling thing.
paragraphs.cn: 你从来不说"我不知道"。你知道吗？这很能说明问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Well, you know, it's hard to say, because -- and this is why it's hard to say. No. 1, I think the Republican primary contests have been about who can be most loyal or who can wrap their arms most around Donald Trump. I think on the Democratic side, it has been the generational divide and the ideological conversation. And I think both of those things will look different in a general election. What I will tell you is I think the ice is melting in the Donald Trump cup.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道，这很难说 -- -- 这就是为什么难说。第一，我认为共和党初选竞争的是谁最忠诚，或者谁最能拥抱唐纳德·特朗普。我认为在民主党方面，是代际分歧和意识形态的讨论。我认为这两件事在大选中都会有所不同。我要告诉你的是，我认为唐纳德·特朗普的冰正在融化。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: I think he's had a chilling effect on the Republican primaries. But we saw in Iowa, we saw in Georgia, and we've seen with some --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为他对共和党初选产生了寒蝉效应。但我们在爱荷华州、佐治亚州看到，也看到一些 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But is that, then, why, then, their candidates would turn to those kinds of messages that do resonate instead of focusing on, here's what Trump did. This is a referendum on him. Let's have a fight about the Iran War. What they're saying is, hey, remember how you hate those people and that people, and these people shouldn't come over the border? We should go back to that conversation.
paragraphs.cn: 但这是否就是为什么他们的候选人会转向那些确实能引起共鸣的信息，而不是关注特朗普做了什么？这是对他的公投。让我们就伊朗战争进行辩论。他们说的是，嘿，记得你有多恨那些人，那些人，这些人不应该越过边境吗？我们应该回到那个话题。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Well, I think that they cannot run away from the reality that Donald Trump's economy has been a failure for them. Voters are going to vote on affordability and fuel and economics you and I can feel. And I think they failed on that. If you look at Georgia, you look at Oklahoma, you look at what is likely going to happen in South Carolina. You look at what happened in Iowa just last week or the week before last.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为他们无法逃避现实，即特朗普的经济对他们来说是失败的。选民会根据我们都能感受到的负担能力、燃料和经济来投票。我认为他们在这些方面失败了。如果你看看佐治亚州、俄克拉荷马州，看看南卡罗来纳州可能发生的事情，再看看上周或上上周爱荷华州发生的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: The narrative has been the same: farmers, rural voters, those who voted for Trump are feeling the effect of a Donald Trump failing economy.
paragraphs.cn: 说法都是一样的：农民、农村选民、那些投票给特朗普的人，正在感受到唐纳德·特朗普失败经济的影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And here's where it gets challenging for -- for your party, Antjuan, is you guys are going to have a really hard time arguing about affordability when you can't get a single Democrat in the House or the Senate to vote against taxpayer-funded sex-change procedures, sex-rejection procedures. So, these --
paragraphs.cn: 而这对你的政党来说是个挑战，安特万，当你们在众议院或参议院找不到一个民主党人投票反对纳税人资助的变性手术、性别转换手术时，你们将很难在负担能力问题上进行辩论。所以，这些 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: These procedures are hundreds of thousands of dollars, and you're going to spend taxpayers -- you're going to tax us over that?
paragraphs.cn: 这些手术花费数十万美元，你们要用纳税人的钱 -- -- 你们要为此向我们征税吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: We're not -- We're not going to distract from the reality that everyday Americans --
paragraphs.cn: 我们不会 -- -- 我们不会偏离现实，即普通美国人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: That is reality. Affordability.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是现实。负担能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: You all come on programs like this, and you want to distract on these issues that Americans are not talking about.
paragraphs.cn: 你们上这种节目，就想转移话题，讨论美国人根本不关心的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: You're talking tax dollars.
paragraphs.cn: 你在谈纳税人的钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: You cannot find one American in this country who's not screaming and yelling about the prices of gas, goods, and services. That's --
paragraphs.cn: 在这个国家，你找不到一个不在为汽油、商品和服务的价格尖叫和抱怨的美国人。那是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: That's what the American people are focused on.
paragraphs.cn: 这才是美国人民关注的焦点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me let you guys --
paragraphs.cn: 让我让你们 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: And that's why you all have failed. That's why you all have failed --
paragraphs.cn: 这就是你们失败的原因。这就是你们失败的原因 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: We haven't failed anything.
paragraphs.cn: 我们没有任何失败。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: -- at the ballot box since Donald Trump has taken off.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Sejak Donald Trump menjabat, di depan kotak suara.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me just pause for a second, because we weren't talking about trans health care. And I feel like that -- we're going on a journey here, Terry.
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya berhenti sejenak, karena kita tidak sedang membahas perawatan medis transgender. Saya rasa kita sedang memulai sebuah perjalanan, Terry.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: It's just a distraction.
paragraphs.cn: Ini hanya gangguan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, let's -- let's journey back to the news today. After the break on CNN THIS MORNING, the votes are being counted in Washington, D.C.'s mayoral primary. Why Trump's influence is looming over that race, too. And he's been off-air for nearly a month. And we're now learning how much Colbert's final gag is costing CBS. In the meantime, good morning to New Yorkers. Live look at the city skyline.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, mari kita kembali ke berita hari ini. Setelah jeda di acara CNN This Morning, suara pemilihan pendahuluan walikota Washington DC sedang dihitung. Mengapa pengaruh Trump juga membayangi kampanye ini? Dia sudah hampir sebulan tidak tampil di layar. Kami sekarang tahu berapa biaya yang harus dibayar CBS untuk klip lucu terakhir Colbert. Sementara itu, selamat pagi warga New York. Ini adalah tampilan langsung cakrawala kota.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Hey, it's now 24 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. The officer involved in the fatal shooting of a 1-year-old boy outside a Mississippi Walmart is now on administrative leave. At least, that's according to reporting from our affiliate in Jackson, Mississippi. Protesters gathering outside the store were hit with tear gas last night. The shooting happened on Sunday.
paragraphs.cn: Hei, sekarang 24 menit setelah jam. Ini adalah ringkasan berita pagi Anda. Seorang petugas polisi yang terlibat dalam penembakan seorang balita laki-laki berusia satu tahun di luar Walmart di Mississippi telah diskors secara administratif. Setidaknya, menurut laporan afiliasi kami di Jackson, Mississippi. Tadi malam, para pengunjuk rasa yang berkumpul di luar toko diserang dengan gas air mata. Penembakan terjadi pada hari Minggu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Officials say the officer was responding to a shoplifting call when two adults ran off with the child into a vehicle, and police claim they then drove towards officers. And that's when an officer opened fire. Now, the people in the car were driven to the hospital, where the baby, Kohen Wiley, was pronounced dead. Officials say they have not publicly identified the officer involved just yet.
paragraphs.cn: Pejabat mengatakan, saat itu petugas sedang merespons laporan pencurian di toko, dua orang dewasa bersama seorang anak berlari ke dalam mobil, dan polisi mengklaim mereka kemudian melaju ke arah petugas. Saat itulah seorang petugas melepaskan tembakan. Sekarang, orang-orang di dalam mobil dibawa ke rumah sakit, dan bayi Kohen Wiley dinyatakan meninggal. Pejabat mengatakan mereka belum mengidentifikasi petugas yang terlibat secara publik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And Democratic state lawmaker Aisha Wahab will advance to a special general election in August in California to fill the House seat vacated by Eric Swalwell. Now, she defeated Melissa Hernandez by more than a 2-to-1 margin. And in Washington, D.C., council member and Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George leads former council member Kenyan McDuffie by 16 points, with 64 percent of the vote counted. And a dramatic rescue caught on camera after a plane crashes into a moving car on a highway in Texas.
paragraphs.cn: Anggota DPR negara bagian dari Partai Demokrat, Aisha Wahab, akan mengikuti pemilihan khusus di California pada bulan Agustus untuk mengisi kursi DPR yang ditinggalkan oleh Eric Swalwell. Dia mengalahkan Melissa Hernandez dengan rasio lebih dari 2 banding 1. Di Washington DC, anggota dewan kota dan sosialis demokrat, Janice Lewis George, unggul 16 poin persentase atas mantan anggota dewan Kenyon McDuffie, dengan 64% suara telah dihitung. Ada juga penyelamatan dramatis yang terekam kamera: sebuah pesawat menabrak mobil yang sedang melaju di jalan raya di Texas.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: At least one person has been killed. Witnesses were seen trying to break a window to get passengers out of the burning aircraft, and police say they received an emergency call from a flight tower last night after the small jet reported mechanical issues. A flight-tracking website shows the plane's signal cut out about two miles from Laredo Airport. And the GOAT makes history again on the world stage. Argentina's Lionel Messi scoring his first World Cup hat trick in the team's tournament opener last night.
paragraphs.cn: Setidaknya satu orang tewas. Saksi mata mencoba memecahkan jendela untuk mengeluarkan penumpang dari pesawat yang terbakar, polisi mengatakan mereka menerima panggilan darurat dari menara kontrol lalu lintas udara tadi malam setelah pesawat jet kecil itu melaporkan masalah mekanis. Sebuah situs pelacakan penerbangan menunjukkan sinyal pesawat menghilang sekitar dua mil dari Bandara Laredo. GOAT kembali membuat sejarah di panggung dunia. Lionel Messi dari Argentina mencetak hat-trick pertamanya di Piala Dunia dalam pertandingan pembuka tim tadi malam.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He led the defending World Cup champs to a three-zero victory over Algeria. This was in Kansas City. Now, with those goals last night, Messi is now tied for the all-time World Cup scoring record with 16 total goals.
paragraphs.cn: Dia memimpin juara bertahan Piala Dunia menang 3-0 atas Aljazair. Ini terjadi di Kansas City. Dengan gol tadi malam, Messi kini menyamai rekor gol terbanyak sepanjang masa Piala Dunia dengan 16 gol total.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Looking for Julian Alvarez. Clearance only as far as -- STEPHEN COLBERT, FORMER HOST, CBS'S "THE LATE SHOW WITH STEPHEN COLBERT": The owner of the music of "A Charlie Brown Christmas" and other Peanuts television specials has filed four lawsuits yesterday against those illegally using the famous song "Linus and Lucy."
paragraphs.cn: Mencari Julian Alvarez. Bola sapuan hanya sampai -- -- Stephen Colbert, mantan pembawa acara The Late Show with Stephen Colbert di CBS: Pemilik musik A Charlie Brown Christmas dan spesial TV Peanuts lainnya kemarin mengajukan empat gugatan atas penggunaan ilegal lagu terkenal Linus and Lucy.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Looks like Colbert got the last word the last night on his show. His final dig to CBS was having his band play that song, and he joked, of course, that he hoped it wouldn't cost the company any money. And of course it did. CBS, now reaching a licensing agreement with the production company behind the song for an undisclosed amount. And the company is saying that money will be donated to the World Central Kitchen. It's an organization Colbert has donated millions to. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, as U.S.
paragraphs.cn: Tampaknya Colbert mengucapkan kata-kata terakhirnya dalam acaranya tadi malam. Sindiran terakhirnya kepada CBS adalah menyuruh bandnya memainkan lagu itu, dan tentu saja, dia bercanda berharap itu tidak akan membuat perusahaan rugi. Tentu saja, itu memang rugi. CBS sekarang telah mencapai kesepakatan lisensi dengan perusahaan yang memproduksi lagu tersebut, dengan jumlah yang tidak diungkapkan. Perusahaan itu mengatakan uang itu akan disumbangkan ke World Central Kitchen. Colbert telah menyumbangkan jutaan dolar ke organisasi itu. Selanjutnya di CNN This Morning, saat Amerika

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: and Iranian officials try to negotiate an end to this war, U.S. intelligence is warning that Iran still has options. And one of them could send oil prices soaring again. Plus, investigators say arrests were made after a foiled attack plot on the White House fight night. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: dan pejabat Iran berusaha mengakhiri perang ini melalui negosiasi, intelijen AS memperingatkan bahwa Iran masih memiliki banyak pilihan. Salah satunya bisa kembali menaikkan harga minyak. Selain itu, penyelidik mengatakan beberapa orang telah ditangkap setelah upaya pembunuhan yang digagalkan di malam pertarungan Gedung Putih.

vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms and details of an understanding between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nota kesepahaman ini bernada menenangkan.

vocabulary.en: the policy of giving in to demands in order to avoid conflict, often with negative connotation
vocabulary.excerpt: This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nota kesepahaman ini bernada menenangkan.

vocabulary.en: to suggest or indicate something, especially something unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这份谅解备忘录带有绥靖意味。

vocabulary.en: a person who is present at an event but does not take part
vocabulary.excerpt: Bystanders race to save passengers after a fiery plane crash.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 飞机起火坠毁后，路人争相救援乘客。

vocabulary.en: burning or containing fire; very hot
vocabulary.excerpt: Bystanders race to save passengers after a fiery plane crash.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 飞机起火坠毁后，路人争相救援乘客。

vocabulary.cn: 令人不寒而栗的；恐怖的
vocabulary.en: frightening or disturbing, especially because of the possibility of violence or harm
vocabulary.excerpt: Five people accused in a chilling plot to attack fight night on the White House lawn.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 五人被指控策划在白宫草坪袭击拳击之夜，令人不寒而栗。

vocabulary.en: to succeed in doing something difficult or unexpected
vocabulary.excerpt: How close were the suspects to actually pulling it off?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 嫌疑人距离真正实施计划有多近？

vocabulary.en: elections held in the middle of a president's term, for Congress and other offices
vocabulary.excerpt: I have no question or doubt that we will win the midterms.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我毫不怀疑我们将赢得中期选举。

vocabulary.cn: 文化战争（社会价值观冲突）
vocabulary.en: conflicts between groups with different cultural values, often in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: a new CNN poll suggests culture wars could play an even bigger role this time around.
vocabulary.exampleCn: CNN最新民调显示，文化战争这次可能扮演更重要的角色。

vocabulary.en: a rapid increase in the population of algae in water, often causing discoloration
vocabulary.excerpt: Was a blue paint job no match for an algae bloom?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 蓝色油漆涂层是否敌不过藻华？

vocabulary.cn: 对某人隐瞒；不让某人知情
vocabulary.en: to keep someone uninformed or unaware of something
vocabulary.excerpt: He needs to stop keeping America in the dark.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他需要停止对美国人民隐瞒。

vocabulary.en: to control or influence the way a story is presented and understood
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump and his administration are attempting to shape the narrative around their proposal with Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普总统及其政府正试图围绕与伊朗的提案塑造叙事。

vocabulary.en: a situation in which people blame each other for a problem
vocabulary.excerpt: We're going to start with the blame game this morning.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 今天早上我们先从互相指责开始。

vocabulary.cn: 微妙的；需要谨慎处理的
vocabulary.en: requiring careful handling, especially because of potential consequences
vocabulary.excerpt: There are some delicate diplomatic things going on.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 有一些微妙的外交事务正在进行。

vocabulary.en: to arrange things in a particular order
vocabulary.excerpt: have asked us to sequence this in the right way.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 要求我们以正确的方式安排顺序。

vocabulary.en: to be better than what was expected
vocabulary.excerpt: I have to say, it's exceeded my expectations.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我得说，它超出了我的预期。

vocabulary.cn: 改变游戏规则的事物；颠覆性因素
vocabulary.en: something that significantly alters the current situation or outcome
vocabulary.excerpt: I think it's a game changer.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Saya pikir ini adalah faktor yang mengubah permainan.

vocabulary.en: to reduce the severity or urgency of a crisis
vocabulary.excerpt: there is something that has relieved the immediate crisis.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ada sesuatu yang meredakan krisis yang ada di depan mata.

vocabulary.en: a long, difficult, and tiring effort
vocabulary.excerpt: that's going to look a lot more like the kind of long slog you saw the Obama administration go through.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Itu akan lebih seperti proses panjang dan sulit yang dialami pemerintahan Obama.

vocabulary.cn: (seseorang) terus-menerus menghuni pikiran seseorang (konotasi negatif, berarti perhatian berlebihan)
vocabulary.en: to be constantly on someone's mind, often used to criticize someone for being obsessed with another person
vocabulary.excerpt: why does he, Obama, live like rent-free in the heads of those working at the White House?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kenapa Obama seperti tinggal gratis di dalam pikiran staf Gedung Putih?

vocabulary.en: a deficiency or failure to meet a requirement or expectation
vocabulary.excerpt: specifically spoke to shortfalls that it had.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Secara khusus menunjukkan kekurangan yang ada di dalamnya.

vocabulary.cn: 预先准备好的；事先安排好的
vocabulary.en: prepared in advance; already decided or arranged
vocabulary.excerpt: he argued that, in fact, that was sort of precooked, and it was on the way.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他辩称，事实上那已经是预先准备好的，而且正在进行中。

vocabulary.en: a person who advocates for war or aggressive military action
vocabulary.excerpt: You have so many warhawks.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你有这么多鹰派人物。

vocabulary.en: a formal event where an agreement is signed, often for symbolic purposes
vocabulary.excerpt: Vice President Vance is supposed to go and actually do a ceremonial signing.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 副总统万斯将前往并实际举行签字仪式。

sentences.en: This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.
sentences.cn: 这份谅解备忘录带有绥靖的味道。
sentences.structure: 主句为主系表结构，主语后跟同位语重复主语，谓语动词smacks of表示"带有……意味"。
sentences.analysis: smacks of是固定搭配，意为"带有……味道/迹象"，常用于负面评价。此句为口语化强调结构，it指代前文memorandum。

sentences.en: Bystanders race to save passengers after a fiery plane crash.
sentences.cn: 旁观者在一场激烈的飞机坠毁后争相拯救乘客。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语bystanders，谓语race to save，宾语passengers，时间状语after a fiery plane crash。
sentences.analysis: race to do sth.表示"争先恐后做某事"，fiery意为"燃烧的、激烈的"，新闻中常用形容词增强画面感。

sentences.en: They were planning to attack the Freedom 250.
sentences.cn: 他们当时正计划袭击"自由250"活动。
sentences.structure: 过去进行时，主语they，谓语were planning to attack，宾语the Freedom 250。
sentences.analysis: Freedom 250是专有名词，指代某个活动或事件，新闻中常使用缩略或代号。

sentences.en: I have no question or doubt that we will win the midterms.
sentences.cn: 我毫不怀疑我们会赢得中期选举。
sentences.structure: 主句I have no question or doubt，后接that引导的同位语从句。
sentences.analysis: no question or doubt是双重否定强调肯定，midterms指中期选举，美国政治常用词。

sentences.en: Democrats have high hopes for the midterms, but a new CNN poll suggests culture wars could play an even bigger role this time around.
sentences.cn: 民主党对中期选举寄予厚望，但一项新的CNN民调显示，文化战争这次可能发挥更大的作用。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由but连接。前半句主谓宾，后半句主谓+宾语从句（省略that）。
sentences.analysis: culture wars指文化战争，美国社会政治术语；this time around意为"这一次"。

sentences.en: And workers armed with bleach and tiny bubbles fight nature at the National Mall's reflecting pool.
sentences.cn: 工人们手持漂白剂和小气泡，在国家广场的倒影池与自然抗争。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语workers，后接过去分词短语armed with...作定语，谓语fight，宾语nature，地点状语at...。
sentences.analysis: armed with意为"装备有"，此处幽默地指清洁工具；National Mall's reflecting pool是华盛顿地标。

sentences.en: Was a blue paint job no match for an algae bloom?
sentences.cn: 蓝色油漆工作难道敌不过藻华吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句，主语a blue paint job，系动词was，表语no match for...。
sentences.analysis: no match for意为"不是……的对手"；algae bloom指藻类大量繁殖，环境术语。

sentences.en: I'm calling on Trump to immediately tell the American people on whatever is in his so-called deal.
sentences.cn: 我呼吁特朗普立即告诉美国人民他那所谓的协议里到底有什么。
sentences.structure: 主句I'm calling on Trump to tell...，后接宾语从句whatever is in his so-called deal。
sentences.analysis: call on sb. to do sth.意为"呼吁某人做某事"；so-called表示"所谓的"，带贬义。

sentences.en: He needs to stop keeping America in the dark.
sentences.cn: 他需要停止让美国蒙在鼓里。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语he，谓语needs to stop，动名词短语keeping America in the dark作宾语。
sentences.analysis: keep sb. in the dark是习语，意为"对某人隐瞒，不让某人知情"。

sentences.en: Right now, President Trump and his administration are attempting to shape the narrative around their proposal with Iran, as the world eagerly awaits the text of this memorandum of understanding.
sentences.cn: 目前，特朗普总统及其政府正试图围绕他们与伊朗的提案塑造叙事，而全世界都在急切等待这份谅解备忘录的文本。
sentences.structure: 主句为现在进行时，主语President Trump and his administration，谓语are attempting to shape，宾语the narrative，后接as引导的时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: shape the narrative是新闻常用表达，意为"塑造舆论/叙事"；memorandum of understanding（MOU）是外交术语。

sentences.en: There are some delicate diplomatic things going on where the Iranians -- and not just the Iranians, but some of our mediators, the Pakistanis and the Qataris -- have asked us to sequence this in the right way.
sentences.cn: 有一些微妙的外交事务正在进行，伊朗人 -- -- 不仅仅是伊朗人，还有我们的一些调解人，巴基斯坦人和卡塔尔人 -- -- 要求我们以正确的方式安排顺序。
sentences.structure: There be句型，主语some delicate diplomatic things，后接现在分词短语going on，再跟where引导的定语从句修饰things，从句中插入破折号补充说明。
sentences.analysis: delicate意为"微妙的"；sequence作动词意为"按顺序安排"；破折号插入语补充说明。

sentences.en: It sets the groundwork for to ensure Iran doesn't have a nuclear weapon.
sentences.cn: 它为确保伊朗不拥有核武器奠定了基础。
sentences.structure: 主句It sets the groundwork for，后接不定式短语to ensure...，其中ensure后跟省略that的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: set the groundwork for意为"为……奠定基础"；注意原文中for to是口语化表达，标准应为for ensuring或to ensure。

sentences.en: It sets the groundwork for a reintegration over time of the economies in the region.
sentences.cn: 它为地区经济逐步重新融合奠定了基础。
sentences.structure: 主句It sets the groundwork for，宾语a reintegration，后接介词短语over time和of the economies in the region作定语。
sentences.analysis: reintegration意为"重新整合"；over time表示"随着时间的推移"。

sentences.en: I was surprised to see Carney, who has not been the most sort of positive relationship with Trump, speak highly of it.
sentences.cn: 我很惊讶看到卡尼 -- -- 他与特朗普的关系并非最积极 -- -- 高度赞扬了它。
sentences.structure: 主句I was surprised to see Carney...speak highly of it，中间插入who引导的非限制性定语从句修饰Carney。
sentences.analysis: speak highly of意为"高度赞扬"；not the most sort of是口语化表达，意为"并非最……的"。

sentences.en: What wouldn't happen immediately, though, is any actual resolution of the nuclear issues.
sentences.cn: 然而，不会立即发生的是核问题的任何实际解决。
sentences.structure: 主语从句What wouldn't happen immediately，系动词is，表语any actual resolution...。
sentences.analysis: though作插入语表示转折；resolution of the nuclear issues意为"核问题的解决"。

sentences.en: That all goes to a second negotiation, one that I think is going to be a lot more difficult, though the president said yesterday he thought it would be relatively easy.
sentences.cn: 这一切都进入第二轮谈判，我认为这一轮谈判将困难得多，尽管总统昨天表示他认为会相对容易。
sentences.structure: 主句That all goes to a second negotiation，后接同位语one，再跟that引导的定语从句，从句中包含though引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: go to意为"进入"；one that...是同位语结构；though引导让步状语。

sentences.en: And that's going to look a lot more like the kind of long slog you saw the Obama administration go through in 2015.
sentences.cn: 那将更像你在2015年看到的奥巴马政府经历的那种漫长而艰难的进程。
sentences.structure: 主句that's going to look like...，宾语the kind of long slog，后接省略关系代词的定语从句you saw...，其中包含宾语补足语go through。
sentences.analysis: long slog意为"漫长而艰难的努力"；go through意为"经历"。

sentences.en: The problem he's got now is he has to go strike an agreement in this second negotiation that exceeds the Obama agreement in all of its elements.
sentences.cn: 他现在面临的问题是，他必须在第二轮谈判中达成一项在所有方面都超过奥巴马协议的协议。
sentences.structure: 主句The problem...is，后接表语从句he has to go strike...，其中包含that引导的定语从句修饰agreement。
sentences.analysis: go strike是口语化表达，相当于go and strike；exceed意为"超过"；in all of its elements意为"在所有方面"。

sentences.en: He called me on -- on Sunday evening to argue that, in fact, that was sort of precooked, and it was on the way.
sentences.cn: 他在周日晚上打电话给我，辩称事实上那件事是预先安排好的，而且正在进行中。
sentences.structure: 主句He called me...to argue，后接that引导的宾语从句，从句为并列句，由and连接。
sentences.analysis: precooked原意为"预煮的"，此处比喻"预先安排好的"；on the way意为"在进行中"。

sentences.en: Already, there are various versions of this agreement, some of which look pretty close to right to me, based on my own reporting, that are floating around on the Internet.
sentences.cn: 已经有这个协议的各种版本，其中一些根据我自己的报道看起来相当准确，这些版本在互联网上流传。
sentences.structure: There be句型，主语various versions，后接some of which引导的非限制性定语从句，从句中包含插入语based on my own reporting和that引导的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: floating around意为"流传"；based on意为"基于"。

topics.title: 美伊谅解备忘录（MOU）争议
topics.content: 美国与伊朗达成一份谅解备忘录（Memorandum of Understanding, MOU），但文本未公开。副总统万斯称因外交原因暂不公布，以色列请求查看被拒。加拿大总理卡尼透露已阅并称赞。分析人士指出协议分两步：先解冻石油贸易缓解危机，核问题留待后续谈判，类似2015年《伊核协议》（JCPOA）谈判。

topics.title: 特朗普背书效力受质疑
topics.content: 特朗普声称其背书（endorsement）是"金标准"（gold standard），但在2026年初选中表现参差。佐治亚州参议员候选人迈克·柯林斯获胜，但其他候选人失利，引发对其政治影响力的讨论。

topics.title: 得州小型飞机坠毁与路人救援
topics.content: 得克萨斯州发生小型飞机坠毁事故，飞机起火（fiery crash），路人（bystanders）迅速行动救援乘客，展现英勇行为。

topics.title: 白宫拳击之夜袭击阴谋
topics.content: 五人被指控策划袭击白宫草坪上的"自由250"拳击赛（Freedom 250）。美国特勤局副总监称嫌疑人计划发动攻击，但未透露具体细节。
topics.keywords: chilling plot · fight night · Secret Service

quiz.question: 原文中 'This memorandum of understanding, it smacks of appeasement.' 中 'smacks of' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 'smack of' 是固定搭配，意为"带有…意味"，常含负面含义。此处指备忘录带有绥靖意味。

quiz.question: 原文 'How close were the suspects to actually pulling it off?' 中 'pull off' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'pull off' 是短语动词，意为"成功完成（困难的事）"。此处指嫌疑人距离成功实施计划有多近。

quiz.question: 原文 'He needs to stop keeping America in the dark.' 中 'keep someone in the dark' 的意思是？
options: A. 让某人待在黑暗处
quiz.explanation: 'keep someone in the dark' 是习语，意为"对某人隐瞒，不让其知情"。此处指特朗普不应隐瞒协议内容。

quiz.question: 根据报道，美伊谅解备忘录（MOU）目前处于什么状态？
options: B. 文本未公开，但部分领导人已阅
C. 以色列已收到副本
quiz.explanation: 报道指出MOU文本未公开，但加拿大总理卡尼称已通过消息来源看到，而以色列请求被拒。

quiz.question: 分析人士认为美伊协议的第一阶段主要目的是什么？
quiz.explanation: David Sanger指出第一阶段是让霍尔木兹海峡的石油重新流动，缓解眼前危机，核问题留待第二阶段。

quiz.question: 关于特朗普的背书，报道指出什么？
options: A. 所有候选人都获胜
B. 在佐治亚州参议员初选中获胜
D. 被民主党候选人超越
quiz.explanation: 报道称特朗普在佐治亚州支持的迈克·柯林斯赢得共和党初选，但整体表现参差，并非全胜。
